summary: Backend-only in-process pytest for the NEW `_auto_detect_manual_position()` running-mode broker poll (anti-stacking guard). Created /app/backend/tests/test_auto_detect_manual_position.py with 11 tests covering every decision point in the review_request: (1) while running & flat & no pending_adoption & broker SHORT(-65) -> pending_adoption={qty:65, side:'SHORT', netqty:-65, avgprice:24000, declined:False} with a 'warning' level alert ('SHORT' and '65' both present in alert.msg), engine.position stays None, broker.place_limit_order NEVER invoked (wired as hard-fail spy); (2) THROTTLE: two rapid back-to-back calls -> broker.get_net_position invoked exactly ONCE (counter-spy verified); (3) extra throttle test with broker FLAT path (pending stays None, but 2nd call still blocked by 30s window); (4) NO-OP when engine.position already tracked (spy.calls==0); (5) NO-OP when pending_adoption already set (spy.calls==0, existing pending untouched); (6) NO-OP when broker.connected=False (spy.calls==0); (7) LONG(+50) -> pending_adoption.side='LONG', alert.level='error'; (8) broker FLAT (netqty=0) leaves pending_adoption=None; (9) broker not_found leaves pending_adoption=None; (10) once pending_adoption is set, engine._entries_blocked() returns True (anti-stacking guard) — verified _entries_blocked()==False before, ==True after; (11) adopt_position(True) on the auto-detected SHORT promotes pending_adoption to engine.position (qty:65 @ 24000, entry_order_id='ADOPTED_MANUAL'), clears pending_adoption, NO order placed (place_limit_order hard-fail spy never tripped). engine._last_pos_check reset to 0.0 in autouse fixture per agent_to_agent_context_note. Regression: re-ran 10 prior pytest suites (test_adopt_on_start, test_broker_pnl, test_exit_retry_throttle, test_expiry_auto_roll, test_feature_batch, test_market_freeze, test_reconcile_sync, test_renko_bot[skipped — hits live API], test_rollover_position[merged into expiry/feature batch], test_security_fixes, test_session_health_guard) — 133 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failures. test_renko_bot.py was excluded from regression because it hits the live /api endpoints (would start/stop the live bot) — same exclusion used in iteration_15. /api/bot/start was NEVER invoked, no live Angel One calls, no orders placed.
critical_code_review_comments: _auto_detect_manual_position() correctly guards with `if self.position is not None or self.pending_adoption is not None: return` BEFORE updating _last_pos_check — this is the right order: a NO-OP path must NOT consume the throttle window, otherwise after declining adoption and manually closing the broker position, the next legitimate detection could be silently delayed by 30s. Verified.

Throttle update (`self._last_pos_check = now`) happens BEFORE the broker call. Good — if get_net_position raises, the next attempt is still throttled (prevents tight retry loops on a flaky broker). However: the function does NOT wrap the broker call in try/except, so a broker exception will propagate up into run_loop. Recommend wrapping with try/except + warning alert for resilience (consistent with how reconcile_sync handles broker failures).

30s throttle is hard-coded as a magic number in two places (lines 692 of server.py). Extract to a class constant `POSITION_CHECK_INTERVAL_S = 30` for clarity and to enable test-time override without monkey-patching time.time().

_auto_detect_manual_position is invoked from run_loop only on the CONNECTED market-open branch (line 1002). On a disconnect/reconnect, the FIRST call after reconnection will fire immediately (since _last_pos_check is not bumped during disconnected window) — this is the correct behaviour (catch stale manual positions ASAP after reconnect).

LONG detection path does NOT log to broker alerts with as much context as SHORT (no 'manual trade?' phrasing). Cosmetic; consider unifying the alert phrasing between _on_start and _auto_detect_manual_position so the user sees the same message regardless of when the position was opened.

server.py is now 1406 lines (>700 guideline). Carry-over from prior iterations. Consider extracting broker reconciliation/adoption/auto-detect into a separate `adoption.py` module — _on_start, _auto_detect_manual_position, and adopt_position share ~70% of their structure.
FastAPI on_event('startup'/'shutdown') deprecation warnings persist (carry-over, not introduced by this feature).
test_credentials: n/a (no app auth; broker.get_net_position & broker.place_limit_order mocked, /api/bot/start NOT invoked per agent_to_agent_context_note)
seed_data_creation: None. All engine state set directly via autouse monkeypatch fixture (engine.running=True, position=None, pending_adoption=None, _last_pos_check=0.0). _persist_state stubbed to no-op.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Anti-stacking running-mode broker poll is fully covered. The 11 new tests use a _Counter spy class to verify exact call counts on broker.get_net_position (essential for the throttle assertion). broker.place_limit_order is wired as a hard-fail spy so any future regression that accidentally places a real order during auto-detect or adoption will fail loudly. test_renko_bot.py was excluded from regression because it hits the live /api endpoints — same as iteration_15. If a future test must run that file, it must come with a guarantee that /api/bot/stop {square_off:false} is called in teardown. The run_loop integration point (line 1002 in server.py) was NOT exercised because that requires driving the full run_loop with mocked time/clock — the unit-level coverage on _auto_detect_manual_position is sufficient to prove correctness of the new logic per the review_request.
rca of the issue: n/a — new feature coverage, not a bug fix. One test-selector bug (alert['text'] vs alert['msg']) was fixed in-line during the first pytest run; the engine stamps alerts with the field name 'msg' (consistent with iteration_15 which had a similar 'level' vs 'severity' fix). No production code modified.
